title: Apply: the fossil record
description: Apply your understanding of the fossil record in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a type of dinosaur called a stegosaurus.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these describes a stegosaurus fossil?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Stegosaurus stenops Life Reconstruction](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Stegosaurus_stenops_Life_Reconstruction.png)" by Fred Wierum, [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)

options.alt: A dinosaur with large plates along its back and spikes on its tail.

choices.content: a stegosaurus skeleton preserved in rock
a drawing of a stegosaurus in a book
a human-made model of a stegosaurus in a museum

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a type of dinosaur called an ankylosaurus.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these describes an ankylosaurus fossil?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[202007 Ankylosaurus magniventris](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:202007_Ankylosaurus_magniventris.svg)" by DataBase Center for Life Science (DBCLS), [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A dinosaur with an armored back and clubbed tail.

choices.content: an ankylosaurus footprint preserved in rock
a drawing of an ankylosaurus in a book
a human-made model of an ankylosaurus in a museum

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a type of dinosaur called a brachiosaurus.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these describes a brachiosaurus fossil?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A dinosaur with a long neck and long tail.

choices.content: a brachiosaurus skull preserved in rock
a drawing of a brachiosaurus in a book
a human-made model of a brachiosaurus in a museum

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows fossils in three undisturbed layers of sedimentary rock.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which fossil in the diagram is the YOUNGEST?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Sedimentary rock forms in layers, one on top of the other. Fossils found in shallower layers are typically younger than fossils found in older, deeper layers.
So, the youngest fossil in the diagram will be found in the shallowest rock layer.
The youngest fossil in the diagram is the fern.

options.alt: A diagram shows three rock layers. The top layer is labeled shallower. The bottom layer is labeled deeper. The top rock layer contains a fern fossil. The middle layer contains a shell fossil. The bottom layer contains a coral fossil.

choices.content: the fern
the shell
the coral

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows fossils in three undisturbed layers of sedimentary rock.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which fossil in the diagram is the OLDEST?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Sedimentary rock forms in layers, one on top of the other. Fossils found in deeper layers are typically older than fossils found in newer, more shallow layers.
So, the oldest fossil in the diagram will be found in the deepest rock layer.
The oldest fossil in the diagram is the coral.

options.alt: A diagram shows three rock layers. The top layer is labeled shallower. The bottom layer is labeled deeper. The top rock layer contains a fern fossil. The middle layer contains a shell fossil. The bottom layer contains a coral fossil.

choices.content: the fern
the shell
the coral

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagrams below show fossils in undisturbed layers of sedimentary rock.

**Which diagram best represents a mass extinction event in the fossil record?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: A mass extinction event is when many species die off around the same time. In the fossil record, this appears as many fossils in an older rock layer, followed by few or no fossils in a newer rock layer.
In undisturbed sedimentary rock, deeper rock layers are older than shallower layers. So, the correct diagram will show a rock layer with few fossils on top of a layer with many fossils.

The correct diagram is:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagram shows two rock layers, one shallower and one deeper. The shallower rock layer is on top of the deeper rock layer. The shallower rock layer contains two fossils. The deeper rock layer contains nine fossils.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9f3485b595e91c7940726ad67136d93d.png)

![A diagram shows two rock layers, one shallower and one deeper. The shallower rock layer is on top of the deeper rock layer. The shallower rock layer contains nine fossils. The deeper rock layer contains two fossils.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cab272bd7a08bc542600196ba2785ee5.png)

![A diagram shows two rock layers, one shallower and one deeper. The shallower rock layer is on top of the deeper rock layer. The shallower rock layer contains three fossils. The deeper rock layer contains two fossils.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0426cf43bc6e39f6df46a1f8efb8f9b3.png)
